title: DoorDash, Inc. (DASH) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of DoorDash, Inc. (DASH) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1. Economic Prospect Score: 75/100 — Strong Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 15% growth rate reflects DoorDash's expanding operating cash flow (over $2.4B TTM) as it continues transitioning from growth-at-all-costs to mature profitability. Increasing penetration of DashPass and expansion into higher-margin grocery and retail delivery will drive continued outsized free cash flow expansion.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 9% discount rate is appropriate given the relatively high inherent risk of the gig economy model, intense competition from Uber Eats, and ongoing regulatory headwinds related to worker classification, balanced by DoorDash's dominant US market share.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 3.0% terminal growth rate aligns with long-term US GDP growth projections. While DoorDash has a solid moat in local delivery logistics, food and convenience delivery are ultimately bound by population growth and consumer spending limits.

thesisTitle: Beyond Food: The Ultimate Local Commerce Network
thesisParagraphs: DoorDash has definitively won the US restaurant delivery war, commanding a 56% market share. The core thesis now relies on its evolution from a food delivery app into a comprehensive local commerce platform. By leveraging its massive network of millions of active consumers and dashers, DoorDash is aggressively expanding into grocery, convenience, and retail, creating an ecosystem where unit economics become progressively more favorable as order density increases.

Despite generating substantial and growing operating cash flow, the current market valuation prices in an aggressive long-term growth trajectory. The transition of couriers into 'AI trainers' and new enterprise logistics offerings will certainly add incremental margin. However, the premium stock price leaves little room for error if macroeconomic pressures squeeze consumer delivery budgets or regulatory reclassifications dramatically increase dasher costs. The company is fundamentally excellent, but the stock currently appears moderately overvalued.
verdictFooter: Based on 2026 actuals and latest TTM data. Data via Finviz and SEC EDGAR.

faq.question: Why did Gemini pick a 15% growth rate for DoorDash?
faq.answer: DoorDash is exhibiting strong operating leverage. As the business scales past initial customer acquisition costs, a larger percentage of its $13.7 billion revenue is falling to the bottom line as free cash flow. A 15% rate models continued efficiency gains and successful expansion into new verticals.

faq.question: What are the biggest risks to this intrinsic value calculation?
faq.answer: The most significant risk is a regulatory shift forcing gig workers to be classified as traditional employees. This would immediately and substantially compress DoorDash's margins. Additionally, a severe economic downturn could reduce the frequency of high-cost food delivery orders.

faq.question: Does this DCF model account for the company's international expansion?
faq.answer: Yes, international growth (like the Wolt acquisition) is factored into the 15% growth rate. However, the model heavily weights the US market, which remains the primary driver of the company's profitability and competitive advantage.

prospectVerdictDetail: DoorDash has solidified its dominant position in the US food delivery market, capturing a commanding 56% market share and an even larger 60% share in convenience delivery. The company is successfully leveraging this scale to drive significant operating cash flow, turning a $13.7 billion revenue base into over $2.4 billion in positive operating cash. While high valuation multiples persist, ongoing AI integration for couriers and aggressive expansion into new verticals like retail delivery provide strong momentum.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: DoorDash continues to execute exceptionally well, translating its category leadership into robust top-line growth and expanding margins, despite operating in a highly competitive space.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: DoorDash has consistently outpaced peers, achieving a staggering $13.7B run rate. The platform's expansion beyond restaurant delivery into groceries and retail sustains its high growth trajectory.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: The company maintains a dominant 56% market share in US food delivery and an even stronger 60% in convenience delivery, successfully fending off well-capitalized rivals.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: Pricing power remains somewhat constrained by consumer sensitivity to delivery fees and restaurant pushback on commission rates, though DashPass adoption helps stabilize recurring revenue.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: DoorDash is rapidly iterating its platform, highlighted by new AI tools to turn couriers into AI trainers and partnerships with retailers like Foot Locker to expand on-demand offerings.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: DoorDash's moat is built on a massive three-sided network effect connecting merchants, consumers, and dashers, though it faces ongoing regulatory scrutiny and relatively low switching costs for consumers.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: While multi-homing is easy for consumers, DashPass subscriptions create a modest lock-in effect. For merchants, integrating DoorDash into their POS systems creates stickiness.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: The platform exhibits powerful local network effects: more restaurants attract more consumers, which in turn attracts more dashers, creating a virtuous cycle that reinforces its market leadership.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: The company faces significant, ongoing regulatory risks regarding worker misclassification (gig worker vs. employee status) and antitrust concerns over price manipulation and fee caps.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: As a platform business, DoorDash operates an inherently asset-light model, relying on independent contractors and restaurant partners' physical infrastructure, enabling high capital efficiency.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Market sentiment is generally positive, buoyed by the company's shift toward strong free cash flow generation, though sky-high valuation multiples require flawless execution.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Analysts remain optimistic as DoorDash demonstrates consistent progress toward GAAP profitability and massive operating cash flow generation exceeding $2.4 billion.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: The narrative is increasingly focused on the company's successful expansion into new verticals (like its recent Foot Locker partnership) and innovative AI applications for couriers.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Led by Tony Xu, the management team has executed its growth and diversification strategy masterfully, while simultaneously pivoting the business model to generate substantial positive cash flow.

prospectKeyRisks: Regulatory actions aimed at reclassifying independent contractors as employees could drastically increase operating costs and disrupt the business model.
Intense competition from well-funded rivals (e.g., Uber Eats) and potential consumer pushback against high delivery fees in a tighter macroeconomic environment.
Past legal liabilities involving withholding tips, antitrust price manipulation, and data privacy issues indicate ongoing compliance and reputational risks.
prospectKeyCatalysts: Continued successful expansion beyond restaurant delivery into high-margin verticals like grocery, retail, and convenience (where they already hold a 60% share).
Increased adoption and monetization of AI tools that improve logistics efficiency and provide new revenue streams (e.g., couriers acting as AI trainers).
Further penetration of DashPass subscriptions, which drives higher order frequency, improves unit economics, and increases consumer lock-in.
prospectMethodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored DASH at 75/100 and Opus at 76/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
